meta.name: Lever India emission factors (seed)
meta.purpose: Deterministic source of truth for carbon math. Gemini interprets user input into structured options; this table computes the numbers. The model never invents a factor.
meta.units_note: Transport factors are kg CO2e per passenger-kilometre (pkm) unless marked per_vehicle_km. Energy is kg CO2e per kWh. Diet is kg CO2e per meal-portion. Goods are kg CO2e embodied per unit.
meta.credibility_warning: These are defensible SEED values, not final. Every factor flagged verify:true must be checked against the primary source before submission. A single wrong factor undermines the whole tool's claim of being engine-computed rather than guessed. Treat this file as the thing you defend to judges.

primary_sources.grid: Central Electricity Authority (CEA), CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, User Guide v19 to v21
primary_sources.transport_intl_reference: Our World in Data, travel carbon footprint; ICCT commercial aviation CO2
primary_sources.rail_india: India GHG Programme, India-specific rail transport emission factors
primary_sources.goods: Generic LCA literature for embodied manufacturing carbon

electricity_kwh.label: Indian grid electricity
electricity_kwh.source: CEA CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, User Guide v20 (Dec 2024): 0.727 tCO2/MWh RE-adjusted weighted average (FY2023-24), 0.716 (FY2022-23). Our 0.72 sits within this range.
electricity_kwh.honesty_note: This is the single most important number in the product. The Indian grid is roughly two to three times dirtier than the EU grid, which is why electrification (EV, induction) saves far less here than Western tools claim. Surface this nuance, do not hide it.
electricity_kwh.verified_against: CEA CO2 Baseline Database v20, Dec 2024

flight_domestic.label: Domestic flight, economy
flight_domestic.note: Short domestic legs are carbon-intensive per km because takeoff dominates. Do not apply long-haul efficiencies to a Kolkata to Bangalore hop.
flight_domestic.source: Our World in Data (UK DESNZ/DEFRA-derived): domestic flight approx 246 g CO2 per passenger km. DEFRA 2024 domestic factor is in the same 0.24-0.25 range.
flight_domestic.verified_against: Our World in Data travel-carbon-footprint; DEFRA 2024

train_ac_electric.label: Train, AC class, electric / mixed traction
train_ac_electric.note: Indian Railways is partly electric, partly diesel. Conservative blended estimate. Sleeper / general class is lower per passenger than AC due to higher occupancy.
train_ac_electric.source: India GHG Programme rail factors (all-class avg approx 0.0078 kg/pkm, 2014-15). AC class runs higher per passenger than the average, so 0.012 is a conservative AC estimate that does not over-claim rail's advantage. International electric rail spans 0.004-0.015.
train_ac_electric.verified_against: India GHG Programme rail transport EF

car_petrol.label: Petrol car
car_petrol.note: Divide by occupancy for per-passenger comparison. A solo driver carries the full 0.17 per km.
car_petrol.source: Our World in Data: average petrol car approx 170 g CO2 per km

car_ev.label: Electric car (India grid charged)
car_ev.honesty_note: Approx 0.108 kg/km on today's Indian grid, NOT zero. This is the headline honesty moment: an EV in India is cleaner than petrol but the gap is smaller than the marketing suggests, because the grid is coal-heavy.

mutton_meal.label: Mutton / red meat meal (per meal)
mutton_meal.note: Ruminant red meat is the highest-leverage dietary swap by a wide margin.

smartphone_new.label: New smartphone (embodied manufacturing carbon)
smartphone_new.note: Most of a phone's lifetime carbon is in manufacture, not use. Keeping a working phone one extra year avoids the full embodied cost of a replacement. Good archetype for the 'keep vs upgrade' decision.
